from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into one fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple phrase templates for each predicate
    templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "{s} features {o} architectural style",
        "buildingStartDate": "construction of {s} started in {o}",
        "completionDate": "{s} was completed in {o}",
        "floorCount": "{s} has {o} floors",
        "location": "{s} is located at {o}",
        "cost": "the cost of {s} was {o}",
        "floorArea": "{s} has a floor area of {o} square metres",
        "architect": "{s} was designed by {o}",
        "owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",
        "formerName": "{s} was formerly known as {o}",
        "height": "{s} stands {o} metres tall",
        "buildingType": "{s} is a {o}",
        "developer": "{s} was developed by {o}",
        "tenant": "{s} has tenant {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "currentTenants": "{s} currently houses {o}",
        "address": "{s} is located at {o}",
        "inaugurationDate": "{s} was inaugurated on {o}",
        "birthPlace": "{s} was born in {o}",
        "deathPlace": "{s} died in {o}",
        "bedCount": "{s} has {o} beds",
        "region": "{s} is in the {o} region",
        "state": "{s} is in the state of {o}",
        "website": "the website of {s} is {o}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "{s} was constructed in {o}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "the NRHP reference number for {s} is {o}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "{s} was added to the NRHP on {o}",
        "significantBuilding": "{s} is known for the significant building {o}",
        "governingBody": "the governing body of {s} is {o}",
        "leader": "the leader of {s} is {o}",
        "chancellor": "the chancellor of {s} is {o}",
        "governmentType": "the government type of {s} is {o}",
        "capital": "the capital of {s} is {o}",
        "language": "the language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "the leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "currency": "the currency of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "the ethnic group in {s} includes {o}",
        "origin": "the origin of {s} is {o}",
        "significantProject": "{s} is known for the significant project {o}",
        "foundationPlace": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "demonym": "the demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "numberOfRooms": "{s} has {o} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "the key person of {s} is {o}",
        "architecture": "the architecture of {s} is {o}",
        "postalCode": "the postal code of {s} is {o}",
    }

    def _clean(o: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes from literal strings."""
        o = o.strip()
        if (o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"')) or (o.startswith("'") and o.endswith("'")):
            return o[1:-1]
        return o

    # Group triples by subject to build concise clauses
    from collections import defaultdict

    subject_map = defaultdict(list)  # subject -> list of (predicate, object)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append((t.predicate, _clean(t.object)))

    clause_parts = []
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_map.items():
        # Build verb phrases for this subject
        verb_phrases = []
        for pred, obj in pred_objs:
            tmpl = templates.get(pred)
            if tmpl:
                # Use template without the subject placeholder
                # Remove the leading subject token and any preceding space/comma
                phrase = tmpl.format(s=subj, o=obj)
                # Strip subject if template starts with it
                if phrase.startswith(subj):
                    phrase = phrase[len(subj):].lstrip()
                verb_phrases.append(phrase)
            else:
                verb_phrases.append(f"{pred} {obj}")

        # Combine verb phrases for the subject
        if len(verb_phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {verb_phrases[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(verb_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + verb_phrases[-1]
        clause_parts.append(clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into one sentence
    if len(clause_parts) == 1:
        sentence = clause_parts[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clause_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + clause_parts[-1] + "."

    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. (Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

@dataclass

        "significantBuilding": "{s} is known for the significant building {o}",
        "governingBody": "the governing body of {s} is {o}",
        "leader": "the leader of {s} is {o}",
        "chancellor": "the chancellor of {s} is {o}",
        "governmentType": "the government type of {s} is {o}",
        "capital": "the capital of {s} is {o}",
        "language": "the language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "the leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "currency": "the currency of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "the ethnic group in {s} includes {o}",
        "origin": "the origin of {s} is {o}",
        "significantProject": "{s} is known for the significant project {o}",
        "foundationPlace": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "demonym": "the demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "numberOfRooms": "{s} has {o} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "the key person of {s} is {o}",
        "architecture": "the architecture of {s} is {o}",
        "postalCode": "the postal code of {s} is {o}",
    }

    def _clean(o: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes from literal strings."""
        o = o.strip()
        if (o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"')) or (o.startswith("'") and o.endswith("'")):
            return o[1:-1]
        return o

    # Build phrases, omitting repeated subject when possible
    phrases = []
    current_subject = None
    for t in triples:
        obj = _clean(t.object)
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            phrase = tmpl.format(s=t.subject, o=obj)
        else:
            phrase = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {obj}"

        if current_subject is None:
            # first triple
            phrases.append(phrase)
            current_subject = t.subject
        elif t.subject == current_subject:
            # same subject: drop the leading subject word(s) if present
            # Find the first occurrence of the subject and remove it
            if phrase.startswith(t.subject):
                stripped = phrase[len(t.subject):].lstrip()
                # Ensure phrase starts with a verb (add "is" if missing)
                if not stripped.lower().startswith(("is ", "was ", "has ", "stands ", "features ", "was", "has", "stands", "features", "was", "was", "was", "was")):
                    stripped = "is " + stripped
                phrases.append(stripped)
            else:
                phrases.append(phrase)
        else:
            # new subject starts a new clause
            phrases.append(phrase)
            current_subject = t.subject

    # Join all phrases into one fluent sentence
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        sentence = phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1] + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
